So first and foremost market survey, we have a ton of new uh filters as well as a summary uh data points that we can customize here.

So you can always navigate over there to see those reviews.
We also have included some new metrics that I want you to be aware of.
Uh up here in the customized metric section.

You can include things now like exposure, gross rent, net, effective rent, uh total availability and vacancy rates as well as any kind of leasing activity uh or concessions across your overall market.
Once you have all of your customizations set in place, you then can apply those metrics to this market survey view.
Now, the great thing here is that we can sort and filter this data uh in any way that we see fit.

So if you're curious which property in your overall market has had the highest number of applications this week, you can hover next to this and click it to sort it from high to low or low to high to see how many uh units have been applied to.
So when I click on this property, you can see the individualized unit number, the price point, any concessions that were running and even see the price history.

We also will allow you to see the floor plan histories.
If you're curious how that has been trending over time, you can click on to the floor plan's name itself and see the trends as well.
Uh What I will kind of walk through here is in regards to how we calculate applications relied and leases.
So applications refers to the number of units we've seen taken offline in the last seven days.
Now, after that seven day window in days, 7 to 14, if a unit is placed back online, we're going to count it as a relied unit.
If it stays offline, we will count it as a lease for you.
Now, for any reason, you need a full rent roll of a property, you can access this for your property or for a competitor within your data set.
So when you click on the property's name, you'll be able to see their website the year built total unit counts and then all of the rent roll.
So all of the available units, leased units and then a report of both.
We're going to pull all of the units that have either been leased or have become available since October of 2020.

Anything that you're curious about?
